headline: Workflow automation pulled ahead as the window's leading technique while agentic coding pipelines moved from triage to autonomous pull requests.
body: Workflow automation broke out as the lone leading mover (momentum 145), well clear of a cluster of lagging architecture topics including AI system architecture, MCP, LSP, and subagents. The shift shows up in concrete agent tooling: PostHog detailed a pipeline that converts product signals into ready-to-merge pull requests [7], PAI built a persistent-memory Life OS on Claude Code with a seven-phase build-and-verify loop [4], and Google Cloud demonstrated self-correcting multi-agent systems via its Agent Development Kit [8]. Alongside this, behavioral training surfaced as a counter-theme, with Snorkel showing a 4B model finetuned for tool discipline outperforming Qwen 3 235B on structured query tasks [2]. New entrants percolation, database scaling, and GPU acceleration appeared at the edges without yet anchoring to items.

cited_items.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
PAI: The Life OS for AI-Powered Development
Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
